_comment: SYNTHETIC enriched requirements graph for the theory-eval harness. Conforms to the BASE schemas/requirements-graph.schema.json (domains -> requirements -> {title, description, legacy_components, data_access, dependencies}) and additionally carries the rich fields (business_rules / validations / error_paths) in the NEW OBJECT form {id, statement, ...}. This is the form the enriched profile (requirements-graph.enriched.schema.json) validates and that the compare_graphs rewrite reads by item['id'] without prose regex.

_design.OBJECT_FORM: Rule items are objects, not 'RULE-001: text' strings. IDs follow RULE-/VAL-/ERR- + 3-digit zero-padded, UNIQUE WITHIN A REQUIREMENT (they restart per req). VAL.error_ref points at an ERR id in the SAME requirement (intra-req join the T4 done-check uses to verify a validation maps to a handled error path).
_design.REQ_X: One req that the blueprint maps to a class (XService). Carries 2 business_rules + 1 validation + 1 error_path = 4 rule ids total. The T4 eval pairs this with target graphs of varying coverage to prove 'done' = rule coverage (and that an uncovered error_path can never PASS), not class-name existence.
_design.data_access_colocation: data_access lists CUSTOMER (the entity present in this requirement's OWN domain), so the co-location invariant (data_access subset of own-domain entities) already holds in this hand-authored fixture and is not what trips the evals -- the normalizer-generated output is.

REQ_X.title: Manage customer account lifecycle
REQ_X.description: Create, update, and validate customer accounts against the CUSTOMER master.

business_rules.statement: A customer account must be created with a unique account identifier.
Account balance updates must preserve the running ledger total.

validations.statement: The account status code must be one of 'A' (Active), 'C' (Closed), or 'S' (Suspended).

error_paths.statement: Reject the update and return code 12 when the account status code is invalid.

CUSTOMER.description: Customer master record entity (co-located with REQ_X in its own domain).

fields.name: account_status
fields.description: Lifecycle status code: A | C | S
